content.text:
# Policy and Design Evaluation ## Security Policy | Question / Query | Rating | Comments | Reference | |------------------|--------|----------|-----------| | Are authentication and authorization controls defined for all user roles? | 🟢 Green | RBAC, MFA for privileged users, and admin separation are documented. | [Security-Control-Matrix.docx](https://example.com/security/Security-Control-Matrix.docx)
Section 2.1, Page 6 | | Are encryption standards defined for data at rest and in transit? | 🟢 Green | TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 standards are explicitly mandated. | [Crypto-Standard.pdf](https://example.com/security/Crypto-Standard.pdf)
Section 3.2, Page 11 | | Is incident response end-to-end and time-bound? | 🟠 Amber | Major steps are documented, but response SLA mapping needs stronger evidence. | [IR-Runbook.ppt](https://example.com/security/IR-Runbook.ppt)
Slide 14 | | Are supplier security controls validated annually? | 🟠 Amber | Control clauses exist, but annual attestation criteria are not fully defined. | [Supplier-Assurance.xlsx](https://example.com/security/Supplier-Assurance.xlsx)
Sheet Controls, Cell D18 | **Summary: 0 Red, 2 Amber, 2 Green** ## Data Policy | Question / Query | Rating | Comments | Reference | |------------------|--------|----------|-----------| | Is data classification complete and consistently applied? | 🟢 Green | Classification levels and handling rules are documented across major datasets. | [Data-Governance.docx](https://example.com/data/Data-Governance.docx)
Section 1.4, Page 4 | | Are retention and deletion schedules operationalized? | 🟠 Amber | Retention periods are listed, but deletion ownership and audit proof are partial. | [Records-Retention.pdf](https://example.com/data/Records-Retention.pdf)
Section 5.1, Page 17 | | Is lineage traceable from source to reporting outputs? | 🟠 Amber | Ownership exists for core data, but cross-domain lineage is incomplete. | [Data-Lineage.ppt](https://example.com/data/Data-Lineage.ppt)
Slide 8 | | Are DSAR and privacy-right workflows tested with evidence? | 🔴 Red | Process exists but no complete timed rehearsal evidence was provided. | [Privacy-Operations.xlsx](https://example.com/data/Privacy-Operations.xlsx)
Sheet DSAR, Cell F22 | **Summary: 1 Red, 2 Amber, 1 Green** ## Risk & Compliance Policy | Question / Query | Rating | Comments | Reference | |------------------|--------|----------|-----------| | Is an active risk register maintained with ownership and review cadence? | 🟢 Green | Risk ownership and monthly governance reviews are defined. | [Risk-Register.xlsx](https://example.com/compliance/Risk-Register.xlsx)
Sheet Enterprise-Risks, Cell A5 | | Are obligations mapped to controls and evidence artifacts? | 🟠 Amber | Control mapping is available, but evidence freshness checks are manual. | [Compliance-Mapping.docx](https://example.com/compliance/Compliance-Mapping.docx)
Section 3.3, Page 10 | | Is control testing independent and periodic? | 🟠 Amber | Testing plan exists, though independent reviewer assignment is pending. | [Control-Testing-Plan.pdf](https://example.com/compliance/Control-Testing-Plan.pdf)
Section 4.2, Page 12 | | Are high-severity treatment actions fully funded and scheduled? | 🔴 Red | High-priority actions are listed but several lack approved budget and deadline. | [Risk-Treatment.ppt](https://example.com/compliance/Risk-Treatment.ppt)
Slide 19 | **Summary: 1 Red, 2 Amber, 1 Green** ## Enterprise Architecture | Question / Query | Rating | Comments | Reference | |------------------|--------|----------|-----------| | Is target architecture aligned to business capability outcomes? | 🟢 Green | Capability map alignment to service outcomes is clear. | [EA-Target-State.docx](https://example.com/ea/EA-Target-State.docx)
Section 2.2, Page 7 | | Are integration patterns standardized and reused across domains? | 🟠 Amber | Standards exist, but implementation consistency varies across teams. | [Integration-Patterns.pdf](https://example.com/ea/Integration-Patterns.pdf)
Section 6.1, Page 21 | | Is technical debt quantified and prioritized by business impact? | 🟠 Amber | Debt backlog exists, but scoring and impact ranking need refinement. | [Tech-Debt-Roadmap.xlsx](https://example.com/ea/Tech-Debt-Roadmap.xlsx)
Sheet Backlog, Cell C9 | | Are NFRs validated against expected workload and resilience goals? | 🟢 Green | Availability, resilience, and performance checks are defined with acceptance criteria. | [NFR-Architecture.ppt](https://example.com/ea/NFR-Architecture.ppt)
Slide 11 | **Summary: 0 Red, 2 Amber, 2 Green** ## Solution Designs | Question / Query | Rating | Comments | Reference | |------------------|--------|----------|-----------| | Are solution boundaries, interfaces, and dependencies clearly documented? | 🟢 Green | Context and sequence views are complete for key scenarios. | [Solution-Blueprint.docx](https://example.com/solution/Solution-Blueprint.docx)
Section 1.2, Page 5 | | Is observability built into design acceptance criteria? | 🟠 Amber | Logs and metrics are covered, but trace correlation is partially specified. | [Observability-Design.pdf](https://example.com/solution/Observability-Design.pdf)
Section 4.4, Page 13 | | Are failure modes and graceful degradation paths explicitly designed? | 🟠 Amber | Core failure paths are listed; fallback behavior is incomplete for two dependencies. | [Resilience-Review.ppt](https://example.com/solution/Resilience-Review.ppt)
Slide 10 | | Is testability addressed through contract testing and environment parity? | 🟢 Green | Contract tests and parity controls are well defined. | [Test-Strategy.xlsx](https://example.com/solution/Test-Strategy.xlsx)
Sheet Coverage, Cell E14 | **Summary: 0 Red, 2 Amber, 2 Green** ## Final / Full Evaluation Summary The overall submission is directionally strong in Security Policy, Enterprise Architecture, and Solution Designs. The most material gaps are in Data Policy and Risk & Compliance execution evidence. ### Cross-Category Scorecard | Category | Red | Amber | Green | Overall | |----------|-----|-------|-------|---------| | Security Policy | 0 | 2 | 2 | Amber-Green | | Data Policy | 1 | 2 | 1 | Amber | | Risk & Compliance Policy | 1 | 2 | 1 | Amber | | Enterprise Architecture | 0 | 2 | 2 | Amber-Green | | Solution Designs | 0 | 2 | 2 | Amber-Green | ### Priority Actions 1. Complete DSAR rehearsal and produce timed evidence for privacy-right fulfilment. 2. Fund and schedule all high-severity risk treatment actions with named owners. 3. Introduce independent control testing and evidence freshness checks. 4. Standardize reusable integration and observability templates across squads. ### Overall Conclusion Proceed with conditions. Red findings require closure before final sign-off; amber findings require tracked remediation milestones.
